YouTube Viewing Behaviour

Introduction

This visual data story explores YouTube viewing behaviour based on a dataset of logged watch activity. The aim is to identify patterns in video length, device usage, and viewing time. The analysis uses R to clean, summarise, and visualise the data.

Distribution of YouTube Video Lengths

Histogram showing distribution of YouTube video lengths

This plot shows the distribution of video lengths watched. Most videos are relatively short, suggesting that shorter content is more commonly viewed.

Device Usage and Viewing Behaviour

Bar chart showing average video length by device

This chart compares average video length across different devices. It helps show whether viewing behaviour changes depending on the device used.

Video Lengths by Time of Day

Boxplot showing video lengths by time of day

This plot shows how video lengths vary across different times of day. It highlights possible differences in viewing patterns depending on when videos are watched.

Viewing Activity Throughout the Day

Line chart showing number of videos watched by hour

This graph shows how viewing activity changes across different hours of the day. It helps identify peak viewing times and daily usage patterns.

Conclusion

Overall, the visualisations suggest that most watched videos are short in duration, and viewing behaviour varies by both device and time of day. The hourly pattern shows clear peaks in activity, indicating that users tend to watch videos at specific times rather than evenly throughout the day.
